page: Microsoft Azure M32ls is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the MS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical Intel Xeon 8280L cores with hyper-threading enabled. Operating at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz, the processor includes a 38 MB L3 cache and supports AVX, AVX512F, AES, and FMA instruction sets. The server is configured with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 1049 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core scores, and single-threaded gzip compression, consistently fall into the weak or poor categories. Conversely, multi-core operations, including stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, achieve average tier results. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks also perform in the average range, alongside Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the M32ls offers a balanced resource density for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity and local storage without needing top-tier single-thread compute performance. The tradeoff of lower single-core efficiency is offset by steady multi-threaded execution and average database performance. This profile makes the server suitable for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and multi-threaded enterprise workloads that benefit from 256 GB of RAM and local storage but do not demand high-frequency single-core processing.
description: Microsoft Azure M32ls is an x86_64 server in the MS family powered by an Intel Xeon 8280L processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores. Operating at 2.7 GHz, it features 256.0 GB of RAM, providing an 8.0 GB per core ratio, alongside 1049 GB of Premium Storage capable local storage. Benchmark data reveals a performance tradeoff: single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression tasks are weak, while multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier. Lacking GPU acceleration, this server is designed for memory-optimized workloads. It is best suited for database operations, multi-threaded enterprise applications, and memory-intensive tasks where multi-core scaling and substantial memory capacity are prioritized over high single-threaded performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M32ls is an MS family server featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and 1049 GB of local storage, optimized for multi-threaded database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M32ls is an Intel-based server with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1049 GB local storage, built for memory-optimized workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and local storage for balanced multi-threaded database workloads.

256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
1049 GB of Premium Storage capable local storage
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance
Suited for database operations and memory-intensive workloads
